commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `runWithModelFallback` to detect registered non-Codex plugin harnesses before provider auth cooldown checks and adds a regression test for disabled Anthropic auth state blocking a `claude-tmux` harness.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main checks provider auth cooldowns before the plugin harness run path, while the embedded runner later treats non-Codex plugin harnesses as owning transport. I did not run a live `claude-tmux` scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the auth-routing policy, not a narrow code repair for automation.

target.title: Bypass stale auth gates for external CLI harnesses
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85105.

- detect registered non-Codex plugin harnesses before model-fallback auth cooldown checks
- skip stale OpenClaw provider auth cooldown gates for those plugin-owned transport candidates
- preserve missing-harness fail-closed behavior, CLI runtime bypass behavior, and Codex auth bootstrap behavior
- add regression coverage for an Anthropic external CLI harness blocked by a disabled stale OpenClaw auth profile

## Root cause

`runWithModelFallback` loaded OpenClaw auth profile state and applied provider cooldown/disabled checks before the embedded plugin harness path could use its lightweight/empty auth store. External CLI harnesses own their transport and credentials, so stale OpenClaw auth-state could prevent the harness from starting even though the CLI would authenticate independently.

## Real behavior proof

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 130 tests passed
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 58 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

The new regression registers a `claude-tmux` harness, configures Anthropic to use it, writes a disabled `anthropic:claude-cli` profile into the model-fallback auth store, and proves the Anthropic candidate still reaches the run callback instead of being skipped/suspended by stale OpenClaw auth state.

## Attribution

If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution or include:

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>
